scope: All runtime locations where lexical or adapter-local logic can determine commercial meaning, entity role, query match, buyer fit, or final qualification.
architecture_before: Canonical LLM plan constrained to a closed signal ontology; adapters discover and frequently assign event semantics locally; regex/keyword extractors can emit matched signals; the common qualifier verifies shape, domain, evidence presence and scores but not entity/event direction against the original query.

findings.current_logic: Drops model signal values not present in the v1 canonical signal ontology and adds heuristic signal floors.
findings.semantic_risk: Open-world predicates are lost or forced into the nearest catalog signal; explicit semantic role and relation direction are not retained.
findings.replacement_component: SemanticCommercialQueryInterpreter plus a lossless SemanticQueryContract attached to the canonical plan.

findings.current_logic: Falls back to a heuristic plan when canonical compilation returns null.
findings.semantic_risk: A complex unseen query can silently become a keyword-derived plan.
findings.replacement_component: Fail-closed semantic contract or clarification_required; heuristic output may only propose discovery hints.

findings.current_logic: Uses heuristic first and a closed semantic graph fallback; paid semantic parser is retired.
findings.semantic_risk: Unexpected language is reduced to a fixed list and may appear semantically understood when it is not.
findings.replacement_component: SemanticCommercialQueryInterpreter as final authority; heuristic/graph retained only as zero-cost hints.

findings.current_logic: Requires at least one canonical required_signal and serializes only catalog signals.
findings.semantic_risk: Cannot represent a dynamic commercial predicate or target role that has no signal ID.
findings.replacement_component: Carry semantic_query_contract and open_predicate alongside optional canonical signal routing hints.

findings.current_logic: Routes only by a static signal-to-adapter table.
findings.semantic_risk: Unknown predicates always collapse to generic web without source reasoning.
findings.replacement_component: Semantic query discovery_hypotheses and source requirements guide routing; static map remains a cheap capability hint.

findings.current_logic: Regex matches assign final event_type, company hint, role-like fields and confidence.
findings.semantic_risk: Actor/recipient/provider direction is not understood; a financing provider can be labeled as a funding recipient.
findings.replacement_component: Cheap candidate-chunk detector only; SemanticCommercialEventInterpreter assigns event and roles.

findings.current_logic: Phrase inclusion declares matched signals and can emit a candidate from first-party page text.
findings.semantic_risk: Phrase presence does not prove relation direction, target role, negation, status or query acceptance.
findings.replacement_component: Semantic interpretation and deterministic grounding before OpportunityCandidate emission.

findings.current_logic: Keyword prefilters and fallback extraction can assign matched_signals.
findings.semantic_risk: Lexical evidence may be promoted to a commercial event when the model is unavailable.
findings.replacement_component: Fallback may only return candidate chunks; final semantic interpretation must be present and grounded.

findings.current_logic: Keyword and field heuristics validate marketing/funding/hiring signals and build business_signals.
findings.semantic_risk: Entity role and event direction can be inferred from generic wording.
findings.replacement_component: SemanticCommercialEventInterpreter output plus grounding verdict becomes mandatory for commercial signals.

findings.current_logic: Vertical detectors and role keyword expansions produce confirmed business signals.
findings.semantic_risk: Generic careers language and ambiguous sector wording can become final evidence.
findings.replacement_component: Structured parsers remain acquisition; all unstructured claims pass the common semantic and grounding gate.

findings.current_logic: Structured ATS/parser fields and role matching determine hiring semantics.
findings.semantic_risk: Structured JobPosting is usually authoritative, but recruiter/employer and specialized-role ambiguity still require query-role validation.
findings.replacement_component: Structured fast path may bypass model only when employer, active vacancy, role and geography are deterministically proven; common grounding still certifies.

findings.current_logic: Registry fields map awardee and tender signal into a candidate.
findings.semantic_risk: Low for structured awardee identifiers, but query role may differ from awardee and must remain explicit.
findings.replacement_component: Structured relation fast path plus common acceptance-rubric grounding.

findings.current_logic: Aliases and source fields assign expansion/location events.
findings.semantic_risk: A location mention can be mistaken for target-company expansion or stale/historical activity.
findings.replacement_component: Semantic event interpretation for unstructured records; structured official-event fast path when relation fields are explicit.

findings.current_logic: Checks domain, entity class, evidence shape, buyer_fit, confidence and opportunity score.
findings.semantic_risk: Does not verify original-query semantics, target role, relation direction, negation, rumor or literal grounding.
findings.replacement_component: SemanticEvidenceGroundingVerifier is mandatory before scoring and qualification.

findings.current_logic: Observed signal IDs and keyword-specialized hiring roles drive signal verification and buyer fit.
findings.semantic_risk: A syntactically complete but semantically inverted event can pass publication gates.
findings.replacement_component: Require a verified semantic_grounding contract with target-role and acceptance-rubric pass before publication.

findings.current_logic: Alias sets over business_signals decide whether required signals are confirmed for the UI.
findings.semantic_risk: Aliases can certify an event that does not place the company in the role requested by the user.
findings.replacement_component: UI filter requires the lifecycle semantic_grounding pass; aliases remain display/routing helpers only.

findings.current_logic: Scores evidence completeness, strength, freshness, fit and contactability after adapter semantics.
findings.semantic_risk: A high-quality but wrong-direction event can receive a high score.
findings.replacement_component: Run only after semantic grounding; scoring never overrides a semantic rejection.

findings.current_logic: Signal aliases are mapped into graph events without a mandatory semantic relation contract.
findings.semantic_risk: Incorrectly directed events can be persisted and amplified by the knowledge graph.
findings.replacement_component: Persist only grounded semantic events with explicit entity roles, direction, evidence and provenance.

required_common_boundary.policy: Adapters acquire; semantic interpreter understands; grounding verifier certifies; resolver links; lifecycle qualifies.
